assumptions: The intragroup dividend has been declared and recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable before elimination in consolidation.
The question concerns the consolidated financial statements and the foreign currency risk on that recognised intragroup balance.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model relevant to consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: No hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the baseline accounting under IAS 21 and IFRS 9 without applying a formal hedge accounting model, under which exchange differences and derivative fair value changes are recognised under their ordinary rules.
